$comment: Exact-match wording dictionary. PURPOSE: when two firms share a value, the STRING must be identical so comparison is clean ('5 trading days', never '5 days'). Seeded with format rules + tokens VERIFIED first-party from the Tradeify content map. This bank GROWS, never forks: when a firm uses a shared value not listed, add its canonical string here so the next firm renders identically. Per-firm numbers are NOT in this file — the agent pulls those first-party; this file governs the WORDING/FORMAT of values, not which firm has which.

rules: Never write bare 'days' when a qualifier applies. Use the firm's first-party qualifier exactly: a payout requiring 5 trading days is '5 trading days', a 5-winning-day cycle is '5 winning days'.
Singular 1 → '1 day' / '1 trading day'. Plural otherwise.
Ranges use an en-dash with no spaces: '1–5'.
Whole dollars, comma thousands, no decimals unless the firm publishes cents.
Account sizes shown as e.g. '$50K' in buttons, '$50,000' in rows — match the cornerstone's existing convention per surface.
If a rule is absent, write the canonical absence string (see absence_strings), not '0%'.
Timezone always abbreviated (ET), space before it.

canonical_tokens._note: Reuse these EXACT strings wherever the concept appears, for any firm that shares it. Verified from Tradeify first-party help center.

drawdown_types.rule: Use the firm's published mechanic; map to one of these canonical labels. Detail/nuance lives on the Rules tab, not the comparison row.

consistency.rule: State scope (eval vs funded) explicitly; never an ambiguous bare '40%'.

payout_methods.rule: Method names are proper nouns — match the firm's exact spelling/casing; add new methods here as encountered.

phases.rule: Public phase labels; internal slot keys stay eval/funded/lightning per the build spec.

absence_strings._rule: When a firm genuinely does not have a feature, use these exact strings — never blank, never '0', never 'N/A' (inconsistent).

row_schema._comment: Mirrors cpf-site-manifest.json data_module.row_schema. Single source for both.

comparison_fields._comment: The canonical set of comparable fields. Each firm fills these from first-party data, formatted per format_rules + canonical_tokens. Field LABELS are fixed (identical across firms); only VALUES change.
comparison_fields.label_rule: These labels are identical on every firm page and the category list. Do not rephrase per firm ('Min payout days' vs 'Minimum payout days' is a defect).
